headline: Affordable housing still out of reach for many: PCUP | ABS-CBN News
text:
A demolition team knocks down houses at 145 Calamba St. in Brgy. Sto. Domingo, Quezon City on February 24, 2024 following a court order from Quezon City RTC Branch 101 in 2020. Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News/File 
MANILA — Affordable housing is still out of reach of many Filipinos who do not have a stable income to cover monthly payments, the Presidential Commission for the Urban Poor said in a briefing on Tuesday.
PCUP Officer-in-charge Elizabeth Avila said most Filipinos who do not own a house are worried about their finances and about having a stable source of income.
She said many families have to focus on their daily needs and cannot set aside money towards owning a house.
“Palagay lang P15,000 ang isang housing unit, pero wala silang kakayanan nabayaran ito monthly dahil hindi stable ang kanilang kabuhayan. Isang dahilan ‘yan,” Avila said.
(Let's say a housing unit costs P15,000 but they can't afford to pay it monthly because their livelihood is not stable, that's one reason.
She added that it could also be a matter of "mindset" for would-be homeowners.
"Maaaring mindset din minsan. Kailangang baguhin natin ang paniniwala at values sa pagkakaroon at pag-acquire ng housing unit," she said.
(We have to change our beliefs and values towards owning a housing unit.)
Minimum wage earners in the National Capital Region earn P610 a day, or around P12,200 a month. 
WORKERS IN FORMAL, INFORMAL SECTOR DON'T OWN HOMES
The Philippines has a housing backlog of 6.5 million units, data showed.
Pag-IBIG Chief Executive Officer Marilene Acosta said 40 percent of Filipinos who have not acquired a house are formal workers and another 40 percent are informal workers.
In lieu of a certificate of employment, informal workers can submit a Certificate of Engagement as proof of their source of income.
Acosta said getting informal workers to register with Pag-IBIG can address their financial woes when it comes to purchasing a house.
“We’re doing the extra mile para ‘yong informal income earners and other working groups can become Pag-IBIG members, save with Pag-IBIG Fund," she said, adding all Filipinos who earn income should be members.
Pag-IBIG offers an interest rate of 3 percent for minimum wage earners who will avail of socialized housing units.
Avila added housing programs need to be sustainable and should take into consideration available job opportunities for future residents.
“Kung magkakaroon ng future housing programs, sana sabayang paghahanda sa magandang tahanan at ‘yong township ay kumpleto na lahat doon, mga opportunities at amenities na kailangan ng kababayan,” Avila said.
(If there will be future housing programs, I hope preparations for a good home and that township will happen at the same time, complete with all the opportunities and amenities that the people need.)

headline: 62 pct chance of La Niña in June: DOST | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — There is a 62 percent chance that the Philippines will experience La Niña starting in June, increasing the possibility of more storms ravaging through the country by the last quarter of the year, the Department of Science and Technology (DOST) said on Tuesday.
READ: El Niño weakens, La Niña watch issued
While weather bureau PAGASA expects the number of storms to be “below normal” this year due to the prevailing El Niño, the number of typhoons is expected to pick up towards yearend and into early 2025, said Ana Liza Solis, officer in charge of the weather bureau’s Climatology and Agrometeorology Division.
“Possible po na medyo marami tayong bagyo at usually malapit yung development ng bagyo [sa lupa],” she said in a press conference in Malacañang.
“Historically, during La Niña conditions, yung nasa eastern sections ng country… yun yung mga highly vulnerable areas,” she said.
Government agencies will need to be more prepared for typhoons during La Niña as these tend to form closer to the shore, said DOST Secretary Renato Solidum.
“Ibig sabihin niyan mas maikli yung lead time so dapat handang handang tayo,” he said.
“People should always make sure that they are ready… kaya importante yung LGU at family preparedness level,” he said.
Before the onset of La Niña, the number of storms entering the Philippines is expected to be “below normal” due to the lingering effects of El Niño, Solidum said.
READ: Fewer storms in PH this year due to El Niño — PAGASA
Around 16 typhoons are expected to enter the Philippine Area of Responsibility, lower than the average 20 storms the country logs annually, according to data from DOST.
“Very dynamic yung weather system that’s why we have to keep on updating everyone,” he added.
El Niño monitoring platform launched
This is one of the reasons that the government has launched the El Niño Southern Oscillation Online Platform, a website that can shows which areas in the Philippines are experiencing the effects of El Niño and La Niña, Solidum said.
READ: 37 provinces experience drought as El Niño continues to weaken — PAGASA
“The public can look at the platform to know more about the different sectors… look at information materials to help them prepare for El Niño and later on La Niña, and later on contribute to the preparedness of their communities,” he said.
“There will be a part of the platform that will be exclusive to local government units… so that we can have the same set of information that can be shared to the public later on,” he said.
The website will also be crucial in the coming months as 54 provinces are expected to experience drought conditions, while 10 others will be hit by dry spells in May, Solidum said.
“By April there will still be a lot of areas to be affected by way below normal rainfall. Rains will come by May but there will still be other areas that will have less rainfall,” he said.
The government has identified which deep wells will be reactivated, but are also cautious in the overuse of these facilities as it may affect ground or soil conditions, he said.

headline: House bill eyes 5 days of calamity leave with pay | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — A bill that grants 5 days of calamity leave with pay has been filed at the House of Representatives.
Agusan del Norte 2nd District Rep. Dale Corvera filed House Bill 10182 or the proposed "Calamity Leave Law."
"Filipinos, especially [those] whose main source of income are wages, are very prone to succumb to the devastating and debilitating effects of natural calamities or disasters," Corvera said in his explanatory note.
The bill grants an annual calamity leave of 5 days with pay, prorated on an annual basis, to every employee with at least 6 months of service.

Solidum: Filipinos should be victors, not victims of disasters

It shall not apply to those already enjoying the benefit regardless of nomenclature; those enjoying similar emergency leave of at least 5 days in times of natural calamities, disasters or personal emergencies and those employed in establishments regularly employing less than 10 employees or in establishments exempted from granting this benefit by the Department of Labor and Employment Secretary.
The 5-day calamity leave with pay shall be available upon the declaration of a state of calamity by the President of the Philippines or the Local Sanggunian.
The calamity leave can be applied for 5 straight working days or on a staggered basis and shall not be deducted from the leave credits.
Government workers or employees whose functions involve the delivery of basic and health services, preparedness/response to natural disasters and calamities, and or the performance of other vital services and who have reported for work during the existence of the state of calamity shall be able to avail of the leave starting from the lifting of the state of calamity and until ten days thereafter.
The calamity leave may be used by those stranded in affected areas with no safe means of transportation to and from work; if the employee is affected by disease, illness or injury due to the calamity or disaster, caring for immediate family members affected by the calamity or disasters and urgent repair and clean up of the damaged dwelling place.
The employee may not be required to apply for special emergency leave in cases of suspension or cancellation of work. Prior applications for a calamity leave shall be revoked.
Unused leaves shall not be cumulative and may not be converted into cash.

Metro Manila traffic a state of calamity: Biz group

headline: Pertussis: Doctor urges mask-wearing in outbreak areas | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — A former government health adviser on Tuesday urged residents in areas where pertussis outbreak has been declared to wear face masks to prevent the further transmission of the respiratory illness.
Quezon City and Iloilo City have declared an outbreak of pertussis or whooping cough. Other areas in Metro Manila such as Pasig City have also seen a rise in cases.
Speaking on ANC, health reform advocate Dr. Tony Leachon said people in outbreak areas should "put on their face masks" and observe minimum public-health measures as the Philippines awaits more vaccines against pertussis.
Leachon also said that he would recommend the reimposition of mandatory wearing of face masks in public spaces, saying adults could be carriers of pertussis. 
"Very important also that we cover the nose and mouth because these will be the sources of infection," he said. "Washing of hands is important, just like during the pandemic."
Pertussis, or tusperina in Filipino, is a highly contagious bacterial infection that is vaccine-preventable. It spreads through droplets produced from coughing or sneezing.
WATCH: What is pertussis?
Some 453 pertussis cases — with 35 deaths — were reported in the first 10 weeks of 2024 across the country, the Department of Health said. During the same period last year, the agency only recorded a total of 23 pertussis cases.
Dr. Rontgene Solante, an infectious diseases specialist, said pertussis infection could cause complications leading to death, especially among young children and infants.
The resurgence of pertussis is seen by doctors and health advocates as a setback to decades of progress made against vaccine-preventable illnesses partly due to vaccine hesitancy driven by misinformation and disinformation.
The DOH meanwhile has attributed the spike in pertussis cases to the disruption in routine vaccinations in children brought by pandemic lockdowns. 
"Get your kids vaccinated, if there are supplies in your LGU. So many kids skip routine vaccination during the lockdowns… you have to go back to your health centers because they are free," Leachon said.
DOH urges vaccination as pertussis, measles cases rise
Health centers to remain open this Holy Week to give pertussis vaccines: DOH
DOH: 3 million pertussis vaccine doses arriving 'soonest'
Meanwhile, Iloilo City Mayor Jerry Treñas said the declaration of a pertussis outbreak in his area would allow the local government to access funds needed to respond to the spread of the bacterial infection. 
The city has logged 15 confirmed pertussis cases and eight suspected cases of the illness.
“May amount kaming naaprubahan – a total of P16 million. A big portion will go to medicines and vaccines. And if necessary, we will add more funds,” Treñas said. 
(We have an approved amount which totals P16 million. A big portion will go to medicines and vaccines.) 
In Metro Manila, 111 cases of the illness were logged in the first 12 weeks of 2024 alone — higher compared to the 5-year average prior the pandemic, data from the health department showed.
Metro Manila Council Chairman and San Juan City Mayor Francis Zamora said response to pertussis required more than just vaccination.
“What we really need is to inform the public ano ba itong pertussis. Kahit ako aminado ako na nitong mga nakaraang araw tsaka ko lang nalaman kung ano ang sakit na ito. Kaya mahalaga na magkaroon tayo ng massive information campaign sa mga local government units,” he said.
(What we really need is to inform the public about what pertussis is. Even I admit that I only learned about this disease in the last couple of days. It’s really important that we have massive information campaigns in local government units.)
-- With a report from Raphael Bosano, ABS-CBN News

headline: MMDA lifts number coding from Holy Wednesday to Good Friday | ABS-CBN News
text:
The Metropolitan Manila Development Authority said it would suspend the number coding from Holy Wednesday to Good Friday or March 27 to 29, 2024. 
There is no number coding on Saturdays and Sundays, the MMDA noted in a Facebook post. 

Holy Week, which begins from Palm Sunday and ends on Easter Sunday, marks the last week of Lent, during which Christians fast, pray and give alms to the needy.
Marcos Jr. reminds public to 'have meaningful, reflective' Holy Week
Catholics make up about 80 percent of the more than 110 million Philippine population.
Hot, humid weather expected during Holy Week
Health centers to remain open this Holy Week to give pertussis vaccines: DOH
-- With a report from Reuters

headline: Filipino scientists forced to halt West PH Sea resource assessment | ABS-CBN News
text: Video shows how Filipino researchers in the West Philippine Sea were injured by a low-flying Chinese Navy helicopter. The international community has rallied behind the Philippines to condemn China’s latest harassment. - The World Tonight, ANC, March 26, 2024

headline: High-ranking NPA leader, 2 others killed in Batangas clash: military | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — A high-ranking member of the communist New People’s Army (NPA) and two others were killed in an encounter with the military in Batangas Tuesday. 
According to the Philippine Army, military troops were conducting operations at Barangay Leviste in Rosario town when they encountered an undetermined number of gunmen allegedly led by Junalice Arante-Isita alyas Arya, from the Southern Tagalog Regional Party Committee.
Isita and two others, Bernardo Bagaas alias John Paul, and Erickson Bedonia, alias Ricky, were killed in the 30-minute firefight.
Authorities also recovered several high-powered firearms, 6 jungle packs, and a wire for improvised explosive devices (IED).
Isita has a standing warrant of arrest for violating Republic Act 11479 or the Anti-Terrorism Act of 2020. She was the wife of Isagani Isita alias Yano, who died on July 30, 2023 in Quezon.
The neutralization of Isita is “a substantial blow to the NPA’s leadership and their reorganization effort in Batangas province,” the military added.

headline: Health expert warns vs using urine, breastmilk to treat sore eyes | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — A health expert warned the public against resorting to home remedies, some of which are unhygienic, to manage or resolve conditions common during warmer months.
Family medicine specialist Dr. Ferdinand De Guzman said among the common illnesses, especially during the latter part of the dry season, is conjunctivitis, more commonly known as sore eyes.
He said some would go as far as washing their eyes with urine or using a lactating mother’s milk as eye drops.
“There is this belief pala that the urine would help sterilize something. Actually, dumi na ‘yan sa katawan ng tao na lumalabas. Mas marami ang (ginagawa) gatas ng ina. I will not advise it.  Alam naman natin mabuti ang mother’s milk pero it’s not good for conjunctivitis,” he said
(Actually, urine is a waste product of the body. A lot also use a lactating mother’s milk. I advise against it. We know that a mother’s milk is good for you but it’s not good for conjunctivitis.)
“Kaya ‘pag may sore eyes, I would advise to consult the center kasi ‘yung iba hindi na kaya ng ordinary eye drops.”



(If a person has conjunctivitis, I would advise to consult the health center because in some cases, ordinary eye drops may not work. 
Heat rashes are also common during hot weather among children who spend longer hours outdoors and under the scorching heat. Prolonged exposure to the heat and sun causes the skin to dry which may lead to heat rashes and itch.
Fortunately, heat rashes are not contagious and may be managed by using a cool compress or calamine lotion.
As the mercury is expected to rise further in the coming months, it’s not enough to just keep hydrated. De Guzman says that while two to three liters of water is an ideal intake every day, electrolyte and ion drinks in between would also be beneficial in replenishing those that were lost during perspiration.
Based on forecasts by state weather bureau PAGASA, temperatures may reach a scorching 38 degrees Celsius in Metro Manila by April and May. The heat may also be higher in Northern Luzon and lowland Mindanao where actual temperatures may range between 39 to 41 degrees Celsius.
These figures, according to PAGASA, are just actual temperatures. When moisture is factored in, the heat index may be much higher.
The heat index is what one feels based on the combined effects of temperature and humidity.
On Tuesday, the heat index in Roxas City, Capiz reached 44 degrees Celsius. Butuan City, Iloilo City, Masbate City, and San Jose, Occidental Mindoro meanwhile recorded 43 degrees Celsius.
Forty-two degrees Celsius heat index was calculated in Quezon City on Monday and in NAIA in Pasay City on Tuesday.
These heat index values are already considered dangerous and continued exposure can lead to heat stroke.
When the heat is too much to bear, experts advised the public to remain indoors. Should there really be a need to go out, it is always best to wear light and comfortable clothing and hydrate frequently.
Metro Manila scorches under 40 degree weather: PAGASA
Keeping hydrated as heat index rise

headline: AFP chief visits Navy personnel injured in China water cannon attack | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA -- Armed Forces of the Philippines Chief of Staff General Romeo Brawner Jr. flew to Puerto Princesa City in Palawan on Tuesday to visit three Navy personnel who were injured in the China Coast Guard’s latest water cannon attack on a Philippine supply vessel near Ayungin Shoal.
The injured personnel were on board the AFP-chartered supply boat Unaizah May 4, which was on its way to deliver fresh provisions to troops stationed at the BRP Sierra Madre last March 23.
Two of the Navy personnel sustained injuries in the head, with one of them bearing the brunt of the water cannon attack, almost throwing him overboard.
“‘Yung pressure ng water was enough to throw one of the personnel na tinamaan at nasa ospital. Tinamaan siya, solid. He was thrown to the wall… doon siya tumama. If walang dingding, he could have been thrown overboard,” Brawner told reporters at a press conference in Palawan.
Another Navy personnel suffered injuries in his eye. The three are currently being treated at the Western Command Hospital.
Brawner, together with Western Command commander Vice Admiral Alberto Carlos and Philippine Coast Guard commandant Admiral Ronnie Gil Gavan, conferred wounded personnel medals to the injured Navy troops.
The military chief also conferred wounded personnel medals to four Navy members who were hurt by broken glass in an earlier CCG water cannon attack on the same vessel on March 5. 
Brawner also gave military merit medals to 16 Navy personnel who were part of the March 5 and March 23 rotation and resupply missions to Ayungin Shoal. 
The latest water cannon attack caused severe damage to Unaizah May 4, shattering its windshield and tearing off almost half of the vessel’s roof decking.
But Brawner said the mission was still “partially successful” as the AFP and PCG were able to deliver troops and supplies to BRP Sierra Madre.
”This is the first time totally na pinigilan ang ating resupply boat na dumikit sa BRP Sierra Madre. Despite this, we were still partially successful because we were still able to deliver personnel and supplies doon sa BRP Sierra Madre. Ang ibang supplies ay nailipat naman natin sa RHIB [rigid hull inflatable boat] at naihatid sa BRP Sierra Madre,” Brawner noted.
“But, then again, we are concerned because of the aggressiveness,” he added. 
In past RoRe missions, two civilian indigenous boats were being used, escorted by two PCG ships.
But for Saturday’s mission, only Unaizah May 4 was deployed. This was UM4’s second voyage to Ayungin Shoal.
“For this particular mission, UM4 was enough to carry all supplies and personnel,” Carlos explained.
“The water cannon caused severe damage to UM4 and injuries to our personnel. Kung ano man intention nila [China], hindi natin alam,” he said.
‘OTHER OPTIONS’
In light of China’s growing assertiveness in asserting its baseless claims in the West Philippine Sea, the government’s security cluster convened on Monday to discuss the next actions to be taken.
Brawner said the AFP is in close coordination with other government agencies on adjustments that can be made in future missions.
”Nag-uusap ang AFP, PH Navy, PCG, BFAR at iba pang ahensya ng gobyerno kung ano at paano pa gagawin ang mga susunod na mission such that maiwasan natin ang mga ganitong insidente na merong nasusugatan,” Brawner said. “So, we can be adjusting our operations, pero hindi namin pwedeng sabihin kung ano ang adjustments na gagawin.”
Carlos said the Wescom is studying all possible options while ensuring to keep the peace in the area.
“As far as future RoRes, we are studying all options. We will continue to resupply our troops in Ayungin… We have a lot of options available to us,” Carlos said.
“While we are enraged and disgusted by the actions of our adversary in the WPS, the Wescom and the AFP and the PCG remain committed in the maintenance of peace and order in the area. Whatever actions we take… It has to be balanced with our commitment to keep the peace,” he continued.
Despite the latest incident, the AFP chief said the military will not be deterred in performing its mandate.
”Tuluy-tuloy pa rin ang ating RoRe mission dahil ang BRP Sierra Madre is considered a commissioned PH Navy ship. Ang mga tao dito ay mga sundalo ng AFP. It is our duty to continue bringing supplies there to sustain them in their operations and make sure that their morale and welfare is taken care of,” Brawner noted.
He also assured the public of the military’s continued presence in the Philippines’ exclusive economic zone (EEZ).
“Tuluy-tuloy ang presence ng ating uniformed personnel sa WPS. Nababantayan natin ang ating sovereignty and sovereign rights. Marami tayong ginagawang operations sa lugar na ito, kasama ang joint sails kasama ang mga allies and partners. Marami pa tayong ginagawang operations dito. What we want to assure our fisherfolks is that, let’s say, ang scientific surveys natin, kaya natin gawin nang malaya within our EEZ,” Brawner said.
“Gusto nating ipaabot sa ating mga kababayan na tuluy-tuloy po kami na maglilingkod sa ating bayan. Tayong mga Pilipino ay hindi papayag na hindi tayo makapaglayag sa ating EEZ,” he added.

headline: Marcos thanks India for rescue of seafarers affected by Houthi attack | ABS-CBN News
text:
President Ferdinand Marcos Jr welcomes India’s Minister of External Affairs Subrahmanyam Jaishankar at the Malacanan Tuesday afternoon. Facebook/Bongbong Marcos 
MANILA -- President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Tuesday thanked the government of India for its help rescuing Filipino seafarers affected by the recent Houthi missile attack on MV True Confidence in the Gulf of Aden.
This follows the courtesy visit of India’s Minister of External Affairs Excellency Subrahmanyam Jaishankar on Marcos at the Malacanan.
“I express my profound gratitude to the Indian government for their swift and decisive action in rescuing Filipino seafarers involved in the MV True Confidence incident,” Marcos said in a Facebook post.
Two Filipino seafarers died when Houthi rebels launched a missile attack on MV True Confidence in the Gulf of Aden on March 6.
READ: 2 Filipino seafarers injured in Houthi attack now in stable condition
An Indian Navy vessel that was part of the international task force patrolling the Red Sea rescued the remaining Filipino crew and brought them to Djibouti.
Marcos also expressed interest to further strengthen the country’s bilateral relations with India.
“I look forward to further strengthening our bilateral relations for the mutual benefit of our nations,” Marcos also said.
READ: India renews support for PH in South China Sea row

RELATED VIDEO:

headline: 2 lumabag sa SIM Registration Act tiklo sa Caloocan, Pasig | ABS-CBN News
text: Timbog ang dalawang lalaki sa magkahiwalay na operasyon ng PNP-Anti-Cybercrime Group sa Pasig at Caloocan dahil sa umano'y online scam paglabag sa SIM Registration Act. Nagpa-Patrol, Raya Capulong. TV Patrol, Martes, 26 Marso 2024

headline: Filipino int'l student in Canada falls victim to car theft | ABS-CBN News
text:
Filipino international student Michael Kong could not hide his feelings when the very first car that he bought here was stolen by unknown suspects in the early hours of March 6, right outside their rented home in Mississauga.
The CCTV footage that he got from his landlord showed the suspect driving away with his vehicle. Kong said he immediately reported the incident to the police. 
“I'm the one who usually drives my children to school every day,” he said. “Right away, I knew it was stolen because it's been frequently reported in the news that car thefts are common here in Mississauga.”
Nearly nine hours later, Kong's car was recovered in the Toronto area, more than 20 kilometers from Mississauga. 
He said it was helpful that there was an anti-theft device installed in his car.
“They [car service provider] said if it wasn't recovered by the afternoon, it would most likely have been picked up by a 40-foot truck,” he added. “They would load the car onto it, take it to Montreal, and then that would be the end of it.”
In his YouTube vlog, Kong shared the moment he saw his recovered car. 
He bought the SUV seven months ago, just when his family was about to join him in Canada. But what concerned him was finding one extra key inside the vehicle.
“How they obtained or reprogrammed the car's code, that's what I don't know,” Kong said. “But that's what's scary. So it's the first time I've heard of a third key.”
Kong's ordeal is a similar case faced by many in Canada as the country faces a significant auto theft crisis.
According to the 2023 auto theft trend report from the Equite Association, Ontario recorded the highest number of theft cases across Canada last year.
At a recent auto theft summit hosted by Peel Region Police, authorities recognized that vehicle theft cases have become a significant concern for residents and for public safety.
“The nature of these auto thefts as we’ve seen are now shifting to things like home invasions, carjacking, and use of illegal firearms,” said police chief Nishan Duraiappah. 
He added: “We recognize there needs to be a collective endeavor to protect the neighborhoods that we all serve and ensure that lives in the community are not put at risk."
Summit officials also urged the federal government to create a national action plan focusing on stolen vehicles, enact a law that would target criminal networks, and enhance security measures at the Port of Montreal.
While Kong considers himself lucky that his car was recovered, he said he now fears for his family’s safety. 
“There is trauma every time you see your car,” he said. “It reminds you that at one point, your car was stolen, that someone else rode in your car who was a bad person.”
He also gave advice for fellow Filipinos, especially those who are planning to buy a vehicle in Canada.
“Just be vigilant because incidents like this happen in Canada,” Kong said. “Maybe don't just stick to being loyal to a specific brand because there are certain brands that are really targeted for theft here.”

headline: Kawani ng QC LGU na nangikil ng negosyante, tiklo | ABS-CBN News

headline: US lawmakers tell Marcos: ‘We will stand by you and with you’ in WPS ordeal | ABS-CBN News
text:
The United States Congressional Delegation makes a courtesy call at the Malacañan, March 26, 2024. Joyce Balancio, ABS-CBN News
MANILA — The US Congressional Delegation on Tuesday expressed support to the Philippines as the country deals with the aggressive actions of China in the West Philippine Sea.
READ: Filipino scientists in Pag-asa Island 'harassed', injured by China aircraft-- BFAR
Senator Kirsten Gillibrand, who led the delegation, made the remarks during their courtesy visit on President Ferdinand Marcos Jr at the Malacañan this afternoon.
"We share your concern about China's aggression with regard to many of the issues around the Philippines," the senator told Marcos.
"We stand with you and we want to continue to stand by you, and with you, and to push that aggression back appropriately," she added.
She also stressed that the Philippines continues to be an ally and a friend to the United States, as she expressed concern over China's aggression despite the international ruling which favored the Philippines and invalidated China's massive claims in the South China Sea.
Gillibrand was joined by fellow Senators Jeanne Shaheen, Roger Marshall, Mark Kelly, Cynthia Lummins, Michael Bennet and Representative Adriano Espaillat, who is a member of the House Committees on Appropriations and Budget.
Marcos, for his part, welcomed the lawmakers as he expressed hope to discuss with them the geopolitical challenges that the country is facing.
"But I'm happy, very happy to welcome all of you to the Philippines, and I hope that the time that you will spend here can be a productive time where we are able to discuss further the situation concerning the Philippines and the geopolitical complications that we are facing presently," he said.
READ: Philippines says three soldiers hurt by China water cannon
The visit of the US Congressional Delegation came on the heels of the most recent aggressive actions by the China Coast Guard against a Philippine vessel that was on a rotation and resupply mission to the Ayungin Shoal.
They will be in the country from March 25 to 26 to discuss PH-US relations, promotion of Regional Peace and Security through Enhanced Defense and Security Cooperation and Economic Cooperation, among others.
RELATED VIDEOS:

headline: Random drug test isinagawa sa mga bus terminal | ABS-CBN News
text: Matapos ang random drug test sa mga bus driver sa Parañaque Integrated Terminal Exchange, mga bus terminal naman sa Cubao, Quezon City ang ininspeksiyon ng mga awtoridad ngayong Martes Santo. headline: India renews support for PH in South China Sea row | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — India’s External Affairs Minister (EAM) Subrahmanyam Jaishankar on Tuesday reiterated his country's support for the Philippines amid China's latest aggression in the West Philippine Sea.
READ: India seeks to beef up defense, maritime ties with Philippines
"I take this opportunity to firmly reiterate India's support to the Philippines for upholding its national sovereignty,” Jaishankar said during a press conference in Manila with Philippine Foreign Affairs Secretary Enrique Manalo.
India is among the countries that expressed support for the Philippines following a March 23 incident in Ayungin Shoal that yet again resulted to injury of Filipino crew and damage to a resupply vessel. 
Meanwhile, Manalo announced that India and the Philippines have decided to accelerate cooperation in various domains, including maritime and defense.
“Our countries have a shared interest in a free, open and inclusive Indo-Pacific region. The minister and I have decided to accelerate our maritime cooperation with a planned maritime dialogue with India to be held in Manila... With regard to our defense and security cooperation, we see the defense industries of India continuing to play an important role in the AFP modernization program,” Manalo said.
The Philippines is expecting the delivery of Indian made BrahMos missiles, intended for the country’s coastal defense missions.
Trade cooperation is also an important domain that both countries are keen to expand. Last year, Philippine exports to India exceeded US$1 billion for the first time, according to Manalo. 
READ: India allows exports of 295,000MT of rice to Philippines, highest of 7 countries
Manalo also committed to prioritize Indian visitors in its e-visa system project to allow easier mobility for Indian tourists and investors.
“We've also taken note of India's significant successes in science and technology, space and financial technology, particularly on financial inclusivity. We are keen to begin closer cooperation in these areas. On tourism, I conveyed to minister Jaishankar that the Philippines is prioritizing Indian travelers in its e-visa system project, which we hope to launch in India in the very near future,” he said.
Philippines is set to take over as India’s country coordinator in the ASEAN-India relations later this year. 
Jaishankar, who is on his second visit to Manila, will also meet with Defense Secretary Gilbert Teodoro and the Philippine Coast Guard today. He will also have a phone call with Presidente Ferdinand Marcos Jr.
“My message in the discussions which Secretary Manalo and I had today was that India, as the fifth largest economy currently, growing at about 7 percent annually, is preparing to step up its engagement with the Philippines,” Jaishankar said.
Manalo also thanked India for its navy’s swift action that saved 13 Filipino crew from the vessel MV True Confidence, after it was hit by a Houthi missile in the Gulf of Aden last March 6.

headline: PITX: 4 drivers skipped random drug test | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — The Parañaque Integrated Terminal Exchange (PITX) on Tuesday again appealed to bus operators to keep their drivers in check this Holy Week season after four drivers skipped the random drug test conducted by the Land Transportation Office (LTO) earlier this week.
“Kinuha yung lisensya nila, nakalista sila, tapos nung tinatawag na para i-undergo na yung urine test para sa drug test, hindi na sila nagpakita,” said PITX Head of Corporate Affairs and Government Relations Jason Salvador.
(Their license were collected, but when they were called to undergo urine test for the drug test, they didn't show up.)
But Salvador said their driver's licenses are still with the LTO so they will not be able to drive.
READ: PITX expects at least 1.7 million passengers this Holy Week
“Nakakalungkot ito kasi alam naman natin, pag tumakas ka, isa lang ang ibig sabihin noon diba? Ayon naman sa batas, pag ikaw ay nag-refuse na magpa-test, automatic ma-sususpend yung license mo ng isang buong taon,” he added.
(This is sad because we know, if you tried to escape, it only means one thing. Under the law, if you refuse drug test, your license is automatically suspended.)
Two drivers also tested positive in the drug test on Monday and are currently undergoing confirmatory tests. Their licenses have also been confiscated.
Although he did not disclose the next schedule for a drug test for drivers, Salvador said they will continue to conduct such testings.
READ: Random drug test ipinatupad sa bus drivers sa PITX
“Hindi lamang kasi ito peligroso para sa sarili nila kundi pati sa kanilang mananakay at sa ibang motorista,” said Salvador.
 (This is not just unsafe for the drivers but also for the passengers and other motorists.)
More than 100,000 passengers are expected at the PITX on Tuesday, just like the previous days. The expected peak will be on Wednesday.
Salvador said despite the influx of travelers, there are still enough buses to cater to everyone.
“May mga ilang ruta na nagkakapunuan na katulad ng Bicol Region pero wag po silang mag-alala dahil na-arrange na natin ‘to nang maaga. Meron naman po tayong mga additional buses,” he said.
(There are routes that are almost fully booked like those for the Bicol Region, but dont worry we're arranging this early. We already have additional buses)
PASSENGERS ENCOURAGED TO WEAR FACE MASKS
Meanwhile, Salvador also encouraged travelers to wear their face masks, especially for long trips, amid outbreaks of pertussis or whooping cough in some areas.
“Although hindi pa naman nire-require ng mga regulating agencies natin yung pagsusuot ng mask, ina-advice natin yung mga mananakay na hangga’t maaari, magsuot sila ng facemask pag nasa loob na sila ng airconditioned buses para sa kanilang kapakanan din,” he said.
(Although it is not yet required, we already advise passengers to wear mask if possible, if they're inside the airconditioned buses for their sake.)
Seventy-year-old Alice Dantes Dela Punta and her 76-year old husband were among those who made sure to wear their face masks as soon as they got to the terminal. They will go on a 12-hour trip to their hometown in Albay.
“For protection pa rin natin, lalo na ngayon nababalita yung whooping cough. E mahirap na, naka-ilang ano na rin tayo nung pandemic, another nanaman paghihirapan natin ito, paano naman tayo? Kawawa na tayo. Mahirap din lalo na’t sitwasyon naming senior,” said Dela Punta.
(This is for protection especially with the rise in whooping cough. We've had pandemic, if there will be another one, it will be difficult for us. Especially for us seniors.)

headline: Pinoy caregiver ibinahagi ang Visita Iglesia nila sa Israel | ABS-CBN News
text:
MAYNILA — Pitong simbahan sa Israel ang binisita ng isang Pinoy caregiver para sa kanyang unang Visita Iglesia sa bansa sa 3 taon niyang pagiging OFW doon.

 Ibinahagi ni Bayan Patroller Khyle Gamboa ang mga kuha niyang video sa mga simbahan sa Transfiguration sa Mt. Tabor, Cana, Nazareth, Capharnaum The Town of Jesus, St. John, St. Peter, at Our Lady of Galilee na binisita niya kasama ang iba pang Pinoy noong Sabado, March 23, 2024.
Kuwento ni Gamboa, naiyak at kinilabutan siya habang dinadaanan ang mga bundok at lugar na pinamalagian din umano noon ni Hesus.
 "Pag-akyat pa lang namin sa unang bundok na inakyat ni Jesus na Mt. Tabor, nakakaluha at nakakakilabot ang naging experience namin, iniisip namin ang naging sakripisyo na ginawa Niya sa atin," aniya.
 Ayon kay Gamboa, inabot ng halos 15 oras ang Visita Iglesia na naranasan niya at iba pang Pinoy sa Israel.
 Nagbahagi din si Gamboa ng video na kuha naman niya sa Jerusalem kung saan sila dumalo sa misa para sa Palm Sunday noong March 24.
